core_mechanism: finite-size scaling of the free-energy barrier separating the two minima of a double-well (bimodal) probability landscape to classify a transition as discontinuous vs continuous, with the landscape reconstructed from the system's full density of states via flat-histogram (entropic) sampling
brief_reason: The abstract builds the density of states with Wang-Landau/Lee entropic sampling and then applies the Lee-Kosterlitz free-energy barrier method to decide whether the barrier between coexisting states signals a genuine first-order (discontinuous) transition or a continuous one.
